extension.summary: Browse in Kodi, play on your OPPO/M9205 over HTTP, switch the TV by CEC.
extension.description: OppoKodiBridge v2 is a clean-slate rewrite that supports exactly one chain: Kodi on a Ugoos AM6B+ (CoreELEC), an M9205 V1 OPPO clone as the player, and a TCL Q9L Pro as the TV. You browse and pick a file in Kodi; the add-on hands the file to the M9205 over its HTTP API and the OPPO plays it. Input switching is by HDMI-CEC: the OPPO asserts itself as the active source when it starts playing (One Touch Play) so the TV switches to it, and when playback ends Kodi reclaims the TV with CECActivateSource. All configuration lives here in Kodi — there is no companion Windows app. The OPPO HTTP API is community-reverse-engineered, not an official protocol, and this add-on is software-verified only — hardware validation is not claimed.
extension.news: v2.0.5 — OPPO API rewritten to match the working M9205 protocol (loginNfsServer / mountNfsSharedFolder / appIconType signin / playnormalfile with /mnt/nfs1 + extraNetPath), server resolved from the OPPO's own device list, and socket timeouts no longer crash the handoff. This is the version that should actually play. v2.0.4 — playback monitor reads the OPPO's real /getglobalinfo flags (is_video_playing / is_audio_playing / is_bdmv_playing / is_disc_playing), confirmed on an M9205; fixes the TV being reclaimed mid-playback. v2.0.3 — packaging fix: the install zip now uses spec-correct forward-slash paths (the previous Compress-Archive build wrote backslashes that broke extraction on Linux/CoreELEC). No add-on code change. v2.0.2 — prefilled the OPPO IP (192.168.10.10) so the Ugoos/M9205/TCL chain is fully preconfigured out of the box; just install and play. v2.0.1 — URL-decode NFS/SMB paths so spaces and parentheses in filenames work; prefilled NFS path-mapping defaults. v2.0.0 — clean-slate v2. One chain only (Ugoos/CoreELEC + M9205 V1 + TCL Q9L Pro). Pure-HTTP handoff to the OPPO; CEC One Touch Play switches the TV; Kodi reclaims via CECActivateSource. All config in Kodi; no Windows configurator.
